error.need_llm: Enter either a cloud API key or a local LLM URL.

abort.import_failed: Found a JARVIS config but no usable LLM (no API key and no local endpoint).

init.title: JARVIS — Core
init.description: Step 1 of 4: persona, directive, and conversation model.

data.use_hass_api: Enable home control (lights, climate, locks, etc.)

routing.title: JARVIS — Routing
routing.description: Step 2 of 4: pick which HA areas are bedrooms (for sleep detection), which media_player group to use for broadcasts, and your phone notify service.

data.bedroom_areas: Bedroom areas (for sleep detection during quiet hours)
data.broadcast_group: Broadcast speaker group (typically your home Cast group)

data_description.bedroom_areas: Any presence sensor in these areas fires sleep mode during quiet hours.
data_description.broadcast_group: Used for critical and high-urgency announcements.
data_description.notify_service: Used when you're away from home or asleep.

observer.title: JARVIS — Observer Mode
observer.description: Step 3 of 4: proactive awareness. JARVIS watches your home and speaks up when something's worth noting. Requires a Gemini API key (billing-enabled recommended).

identity.title: JARVIS — Identity
identity.description: Step 4 of 4: per-person recognition, so JARVIS tailors itself to who's speaking. Works with no special hardware using presence and (if set up) camera face recognition. Voice fingerprinting is the most accurate but needs a local GPU, so it's off by default.

data.identity_min_confidence: Minimum confidence before committing to a person

data_description.identity_enabled: Uses who's home + recent camera face recognition. No GPU required.
data_description.identity_voice_fingerprint: Leave off unless you have a GPU server running a voice-ID model.
data_description.identity_min_confidence: Higher = more cautious; stays neutral when unsure.
